  • [ - ] Caseyyy1 - member since 28-Nov-17

    I love the potential in a 3- 4 - 2 - 1 formation. It's not a set up I've seen for any other team and it seems to be such an obvious awesome formation that I don't understand why I've never thought of it before and/or why isn't it already being used by other premiership teams. I can't see Shaw being fit but he will eventually be a great addition to the team/formation. Ideally I eventually see the line up being Yoro, De Ligt, Martinez, Dalot, Ugarte, Mainoo, Shaw, Diallo, Fernandez, and Garnacho or Hojlund. - With plenty of other options for every position. Amorim looks like a perfect fit for Man United. A Pep type manager for years to come, potentially...I do hope so, soooo much....An era as successful as Fergusons, yes please.... Happy face.

    1 year ago
     +1 Reply Report Sign in or join us to rate comments! Sign In || Join Us
  • [ - ] GoldenChest - member since 07-Jan-14

    @Caseyyy1 It’s not new, Atalanta plays same system and sometimes switch to the 3412 variation depending on opponent. It’s great for exposing opponents on the counter and also pressing and defending because when you soak up the press it becomes a 541 or 451.. But for this system to work the right stamina and mentality is required. I think Amorim should do what he did at Sporting, take the risk by scouting the MU academy ranks, flush out any unserious senior player.
